title: VPN Tunnel Stability
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa"
| where action IN ("session_connect","session_disconnect")
| transaction user maxspan=1h
| where eventcount > 4
| table user, eventcount, duration
| sort -eventcount
description: Frequent disconnects indicate network issues, client problems, or infrastructure instability affecting user productivity.
value: Frequent disconnects indicate network issues, client problems, or infrastructure instability affecting user productivity.
implementation: Track connect/disconnect patterns per user. Identify users with >4 reconnections per hour. Correlate with network quality metrics. Alert on widespread instability (multiple users affected simultaneously). Report for helpdesk.
visualization: Table (unstable connections), Bar chart (reconnects by user), Line chart (reconnection rate trend).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-asa`, `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (GlobalProtect), `TA-fortinet_fortigate`, `Splunk_TA_juniper`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: VPN session logs (connect/disconnect events).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Track connect/disconnect patterns per user. Identify users with >4 reconnections per hour. Correlate with network quality metrics. Alert on widespread instability (multiple users affected simultaneously). Report for helpdesk.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa"
| where action IN ("session_connect","session_disconnect")
| transaction user maxspan=1h
| where eventcount > 4
| table user, eventcount, duration
| sort -eventcount
```

Understanding this SPL

**VPN Tunnel Stability** ‚Äî Frequent disconnects indicate network issues, client problems, or infrastructure instability affecting user productivity.

Documented **Data sources**: VPN session logs (connect/disconnect events). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-asa`, `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (GlobalProtect), `TA-fortinet_fortigate`, `Splunk_TA_juniper`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: cisco:asa. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="cisco:asa". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where action IN ("session_connect","session_disconnect")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Groups related events into transactions ‚Äî prefer `maxspan`/`maxpause`/`maxevents` for bounded memory.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where eventcount > 4` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **VPN Tunnel Stability**): table user, eventcount, duration
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with Palo Alto Networks Prisma Access or firewall manager for the same users, rules, and time window.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unstable connections), Bar chart (reconnects by user), Line chart (reconnection rate trend).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
hardware: Cisco ASA 5506-X/5508-X/5516-X/5525-X/5545-X/5555-X, ASAv, Cisco Secure Firewall 3100/4200; Palo Alto PA-220/PA-440/PA-3200/PA-5200, GlobalProtect; Fortinet FortiGate 60F/100F/200F/600F/1800F, FortiGate SSL-VPN; Juniper SRX300/SRX1500/SRX4100/SRX4200, Junos Dynamic VPN
